name: Muscat
tagline: Arabian Elegance, Desert Mountains, and Genuine Hospitality
description: Muscat is the Middle East's best-kept secret — a strikingly beautiful capital wedged between the Hajar Mountains and the Arabian Sea, offering a unique blend of traditional Omani culture and modern comfort. Unlike flashier Gulf neighbors, Muscat is understated, safe, and genuinely welcoming to foreigners. The cost of living is moderate by Gulf standards, there is no income tax, healthcare is good, and the natural surroundings — from dramatic wadis to pristine beaches — are spectacular. English is widely understood, making daily life accessible for Western expats.

quickFacts.visa: 10-day visa on arrival or e-visa for US citizens. Investor residency available with property purchase ($260K+). Retirement visa pilot program launched — requires proof of income and health insurance. Tourist visa renewals possible.
quickFacts.safetyRating: Very High – one of the safest countries in the world
quickFacts.climate: Hot desert with extremely hot summers and pleasant winters

housing.description: 1BR apartment in Al Khuwair, Bausher, or a modest flat in Ruwi

dining.description: Local Omani and Indian restaurants, shawarma shops, casual dining 3x/week

healthcare.description: Basic private health insurance; Oman's public hospitals are good quality

transportation.description: Used car with insurance — essential in Muscat as public transit is limited

entertainment.description: Beach days, wadi swimming, mountain drives, souq visits, mosque architecture

1M.lifestyle: A 1-bedroom apartment in Al Khuwair or Bausher and a used car, which is essential since Muscat has minimal public transit. Eating at local Omani and Indian restaurants a few times a week, cooking at home otherwise. Basic private health insurance. The beaches and mountain wadis are free and spectacular. Summer heat from May to September is extreme and pushes A/C costs up significantly. Manageable but tight, with no income tax helping stretch the budget.

housing.description: 2BR apartment in Qurum, Al Mouj, or a villa flat in Shatti Al Qurum near the beach

dining.description: Regular dining at international restaurants, beach club lunches, traditional Omani feasts

healthcare.description: Comprehensive private insurance with access to modern hospitals and specialists

transportation.description: Reliable mid-range SUV for wadi and desert exploration; essential for lifestyle

entertainment.description: Diving in Daymaniyat Islands, desert camping, golf, beach club membership, cultural events

2M.lifestyle: A 2-bedroom apartment in Qurum or Shatti Al Qurum near the beach, a mid-range SUV for exploring wadis and desert terrain, and dining out at good international restaurants several times a week. Comprehensive private health insurance. Diving trips to the Daymaniyat Islands and desert camping on weekends. No income tax helps the budget go further. Day-to-day life is comfortable and you have room for regular regional travel.

housing.description: 3BR villa in Al Mouj (The Wave), Qurum, or a beachfront apartment in Shatti Al Qurum

dining.description: Fine dining at top hotels (Al Bustan Palace, Chedi), beach restaurants, international cuisine

healthcare.description: Premium private insurance with VIP hospital access and international coverage

transportation.description: Luxury SUV (Land Cruiser) for off-road adventures, second city car, premium insurance

entertainment.description: Golf club membership, diving expeditions, luxury desert camping, weekend trips to Jebel Akhdar

3M.lifestyle: A villa in Al Mouj or a beachfront apartment in Shatti Al Qurum. A Land Cruiser or similar SUV for off-road trips to Jebel Shams, Ras al Jinz, and the wadis, plus a second city car. Regular dining at top hotel restaurants like Al Bustan Palace and The Chedi. Premium health insurance with international coverage. Golf club membership and diving trips. Oman's moderate Gulf costs leave a comfortable monthly buffer.

housing.description: Luxury villa in Al Mouj with marina access, or a premium beachfront property in Qantab

dining.description: Regular fine dining at 5-star hotels, private chef for entertaining, exclusive food experiences

healthcare.description: VIP concierge medical service with international hospital network and evacuation coverage

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles (Range Rover, Porsche), driver available, premium insurance

entertainment.description: Exclusive golf and yacht club, private diving charters, luxury desert expeditions, cultural patronage

domesticHelp.description: Full-time housekeeper, cook, gardener, and pool maintenance; live-in staff common in Oman

luxuryExtras.description: First-class international travel, luxury Oman resort stays, yacht charters, philanthropy

5M.lifestyle: A large villa in Al Mouj with marina access, a housekeeper, cook, gardener, and driver. A Range Rover or similar for desert and mountain trips, business class flights for international travel, and premium healthcare with international evacuation coverage. Regular dining at Muscat's best restaurants and golf or yacht club membership. Oman's safety, natural beauty, and moderate Gulf costs make this a high quality of life with meaningful margin.

housing.description: Grand waterfront villa in Al Mouj or a private compound with beach access in Yiti

dining.description: Personal chef, private dining at royal-standard venues, imported delicacies, lavish hosting

healthcare.description: Top-tier international concierge medicine with global coverage and wellness retreats

transportation.description: Multiple luxury vehicles, full-time driver, private yacht, helicopter access, first-class travel

entertainment.description: Private yacht cruises to Musandam, desert luxury camps, international events, art collecting

domesticHelp.description: Full estate staff including chef, housekeepers, gardeners, security, driver, and boat crew

luxuryExtras.description: Private jet access, luxury expedition travel, real estate investments, major philanthropy

10M.lifestyle: A large waterfront villa in Al Mouj or a private property with beach access in Yiti, with full household staff including a housekeeper, cook, and driver. Multiple vehicles, first class international flights, and top-tier global health coverage. Regular fine dining, boat trips along the coast and to Musandam, and membership at the best clubs. No income tax and Muscat's moderate costs leave a large margin, though housing at the top end absorbs a meaningful share of the budget.
